assessments.status_rationale: Today’s news (CNBC) reports Anthropic was “blacklisted”/rejected by the Pentagon, but the prediction requires evidence of formal DoD-wide acquisition/procurement guidance (e.g., class deviation/DPAP memo) explicitly naming “Anthropic.” A reported blacklist decision could be policy, but the bullet list does not clearly confirm a formal acquisition guidance document was published.
No item in today’s bullets indicates OFAC issued a new Iran-related sanctions package designating ≥15 individuals/entities tied to oil exports or IRGC revenue in a single press release.
While there is coverage of potential oil-market impacts from U.S. strikes on Iran, no bullet provides confirmation that front-month NYMEX WTI settled at or above $100 on any trading day.
No bullet reports the U.S. 10-year Treasury constant-maturity yield (DGS10) closing at or below 3.50%.
The relevant BEA advance estimate for 2026 Q2 real GDP growth has not been referenced in today’s news bullets.
No bullet confirms the VIX closed at or above 40.0 on any trading day.
No bullet references a BLS Employment Situation release showing U-3 unemployment at or above 5.0%.
No bullet reports a Stripe workforce reduction of at least 8%.
Today’s bullets do not show an FTC or DOJ public confirmation of an antitrust investigation or inquiry specifically referencing OpenAI or the Microsoft–OpenAI partnership.

No bullet indicates a publicly announced ceasefire-monitoring/implementation mechanism for a Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant repair truce with a stated start date involving the IAEA or another named monitor.
